channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sony Playstation 5 prices are going up. Here’s where consumers will get hit
item.description: Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia, and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok travel influencers are illegally hitching a ride on Mauritania’s Iron Ore Train
item.description: Mauritania isn’t typically a major tourist destination. But its only railway has recently become the subject of a viral TikTok travel trend: riding the “Iron Ore Train.” This 437-mile journey through the Sahara desert offers dramatic selfie backdrops—and no shortage of controversy.
item.dc:creator: Regina Borsellino, Fast Company

item.title: Bad news, the Fujifilm X100VI may now be impossible to buy in the US –here's what to get instead
item.description: Rumors suggest that Fujifilm has asked retailers to suspend orders of three cameras, including the X100VI –and yes, tariffs could be to blame.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft signs a major new carbon removal deal to try and help minimize environmental effects even further
item.description: In an effort to minimize its environmental impact, Microsoft will buy a further 3.7 million metric tons of carbon removal credits.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta faces historic antitrust trial that could force it to break off Instagram, WhatsApp
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A historic antitrust trial began Monday for Meta Platforms Inc. in a case that could force the tech giant to break off Instagram and WhatsApp, startups it bought more than a decade ago that have since grown into social media powerhouses.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel to sell 51% Altera stake to Silver Lake amid cost cuts
item.description: Intel Corp. has agreed to sell a 51% stake in its programmable chips unit Altera to Silver Lake Management, making good on plans to start spinning off non-core assets.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia pledges $500 billion to manufacture AI chips, supercomputers in US
item.description: Nvidia will manufacture up to $500 billion of artificial intelligence (AI) chips and supercomputers entirely in the U.S. over the next four years, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cisco SVP talks agentic AI, quantum security and sustainable infrastructure
item.description: When responsibility for a company’s long-term growth and differentiation in the market is part of your job description, it can be an adventure. Nathan Jokel, senior vice president of corporate strategy and alliances at Cisco, has stepped up to the challenge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster
item.description: Shadi Azoum, the NAVWAR’s program manager for the SBIR/STTR programs, said about 60% of their entire SBIR and STTR awards take advantage of Catapult. The post NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How people secured their secrets before encyption
item.description: Centuries before encrypted texts and secure video conferencing, people relied on physical engineering to keep their written messages sturdy, sealed, and secure against eavesdroppers.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia says it will build up to $500 billion of AI gear in US
item.description: Electronics players around the world, including chipmakers, are reeling from shifting new tariff policies from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sennheiser launches AirPods-like open earbuds, and if they're as good as the rest of its Accentum headphones, they'll be a bargain
item.description: The Accentum Open are lightweight, open earbuds with an attractively low price and a slim case design
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IT compensation satisfaction at an all-time low
item.description: A recent survey found IT compensation satisfaction has hit a record low, with relatively few IT workers saying they are satisfied with their pay. Industry observers, however, suggest the issue might not be entirely about money, leaving CIOs with options for boosting morale on thin budgets.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US stocks edge higher after markets rally on Trump’s pause for some of his electronics tariffs
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he was temporarily exempting smartphones, computers and other electronics from his tariffs after China announced Friday that it was boosting its tariffs on U.S. products to 125% in the latest tit-for-tat increase following Trump’s escalations on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump Administration's Tariff Move on Electronics: A Temporary Reprieve or a Long-Term Plan?
item.description: The Trump administration's move to exempt smartphones, computers, and other electronics from tariffs is temporary, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick says.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 5 mobile privacy terms you need to know to protect yourself
item.description: The CyberGuy breaks down five mobile privacy terms that could make a difference when it comes to keeping your personal information safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: Many of Silicon Valley's most prominent tech leaders have embraced President Donald Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Summer I Turned Pretty season 3: everything we know so far about the hit Prime Video show’s return
item.description: The Summer I Turned Pretty season 3 officially lands in July. Here's everything we know so far about the hit Prime Video show's return including trailer, cast, plot rumors and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: Across Europe and further afield, counterterrorism agencies are grappling with a new generation of young people who are being radicalized online and consuming ultraviolent content produced by extremist groups and their supporters.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With this simple gadget, you can make great-tasting pour-over coffee with fewer beans
item.description: Brewing coffee is all about extraction, and the right kettle will help enormously. Here's how to choose one.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: White House record-keeping revamp
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 things to know about Meta’s upcoming FTC trial
item.description: Meta is set to face off against the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) on Monday in an antitrust trial that could force the social media giant to divest itself of Instagram and WhatsApp. The closely watched trial carries high stakes for Meta’s $1.3 trillion market value.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Venmo’s dubious defaults look like a permanent privacy foul
item.description: If it weren’t for Signal, Venmo might be the most infamous app of the Trump administration—and maybe the most beloved among journalists covering this White House.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Hands-On Labs: The Key to Accelerating CMMC 2.0 Compliance
item.description: INE Security Highlights How Practical, immersive training environments help defense contractors meet DoD cybersecurity requirements Defense contractors are facing increased pressure to meet the Department of Defense’s stringent Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) 2.0 requirements ahead
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Teen goes from 10 nightly seizures to zero with brain implant
item.description: The NeuroOne OneRF Ablation System is a new device with FDA-cleared technology designed for both diagnosing and treating neurological disorders in one procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defining leadership through mentorship and a strong network
item.description: Andrea Schulze understands the importance of mentorship when it comes to fostering more gender diversity in the tech industry, especially up to the leadership level.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DSPy: An open-source framework for LLM-powered applications
item.description: The past year has seen explosive growth in generative AI and the tools for integrating generative AI models into applications. Developers are eager to harness large language models (LLMs) to build smarter applications, but doing so effectively remains challenging.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Fortinet Expands AI Support Across Security Fabric Platform
item.description: Fortinet has expanded support for AI across its core cybersecurity platform to protect enterprise customers from evolving threats and simplify network and security operations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: The dangers of oversharing with AI tools
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Google Unveils Open Protocol to Connect Agents Across Ecosystems
item.description: Google introduces Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol to facilitate communication between agents, enabling enterprises to adopt agents more readily and automate complex workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World
